std.bytes.alloc: Returns new zero-filled bytes of length n.
std.bytes.cmp_range: Compares a[a_off..a_off+a_len] to b[b_off..b_off+b_len] as unsigned bytes; returns -1/0/1 with a shorter prefix sorting first; traps if either range is out of bounds.
std.bytes.concat: Returns new owned bytes holding a followed by b.
std.bytes.copy: Returns an owned bytes copy of view b.
std.bytes.count_u8: Returns how many bytes of v equal target (bytes compare as i32 values 0-255).
std.bytes.drop: Returns an owned copy of b without its first min(n, len) bytes; n<0 is treated as 0.
std.bytes.ends_with: Returns 1 if a ends with suffix (empty suffix always matches), else 0.
std.bytes.eq: Returns 1 if a and b have identical length and bytes, else 0.
std.bytes.find_u8: Returns the index of the first byte equal to target, or -1 if absent.
std.bytes.get_u8: Returns byte i of b as i32 0-255; traps if i is out of bounds.
std.bytes.len: Returns the length of b in bytes.
std.bytes.max_u8: Returns the maximum byte value in v, or 0 if v is empty.
std.bytes.reverse: Returns an owned byte-reversed copy of b.
std.bytes.set_u8: Writes v & 0xFF at index i in place; traps if i is out of bounds; consumes b and returns the updated bytes.
std.bytes.slice: Returns an owned copy of b[start..start+len] with clamping: negative start/len become 0, and start/len are clipped to the input bounds (never traps).
std.bytes.slice_v1: Alias of std.bytes.slice: clamped, never-trapping owned copy of b[start..start+len].
std.bytes.starts_with: Returns 1 if a starts with prefix (empty prefix always matches), else 0.
std.bytes.strip_prefix_view: Returns a view of a without prefix when a starts with it; otherwise returns the view a unchanged.
std.bytes.strip_suffix_view: Returns a view of a without suffix when a ends with it; otherwise returns the view a unchanged.
std.bytes.sum_u8: Returns the wrapping i32 sum of all byte values in v.
std.bytes.take: Returns an owned copy of the first min(n, len) bytes of b; n<0 is treated as 0.
std.bytes.trim_ascii_view: Returns a view of b with leading and trailing 0x20/0x09/0x0A/0x0D removed; all-whitespace input yields an empty view.
std.fmt.s32_to_dec: Formats x as signed i32 ASCII decimal with a leading '-' (0x2D) when negative; -2147483648 is handled.
std.fmt.u32_to_dec: Formats x interpreted as unsigned u32 into ASCII decimal digits with no sign or padding; 0 formats as '0'.
std.json.canonicalize_small: Re-emits one JSON value with all inter-token whitespace removed and object keys sorted ascending by raw (still-escaped) key bytes; string/number tokens kept verbatim; numbers are integers only (-?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)); invalid input or trailing data returns 'ERR'.
std.json.encode: With opts bit 0 set behaves as canonicalize_small; with bit 0 clear validates b as one JSON value (whitespace allowed around it) and returns the value's original bytes; returns the 3 bytes 'ERR' on failure.
std.json.extract_path_canon_or_err: Input is <json> 0x00 <dot.separated.path>; descends objects by exact raw key bytes and returns the canonicalized value at the path; returns 'ERR' if there is no 0x00, an empty segment, a missing key, a non-object step, or invalid/trailing JSON.
std.json.pretty_encode: Canonicalizes then pretty-prints: 2-space indent, newline after '{', '[' and ',', one space after ':', empty {} and [] stay inline; returns 'ERR' on invalid input.
std.map.word_freq_sorted_ascii: Counts lowercased A-Za-z runs and emits 'word=count' lines ('=' 0x3D, u32 decimal count) joined by single 0x0A, sorted by word bytes ascending; task-specific helper; prefer composing std.text.ascii + std.small_map for general logic.
std.parse.i32_dec: Parses all of b (0x20/0x09 trimmed at both ends) as decimal with optional single leading + or -; result_i32 err 1 = empty, 2 = bad char or no digits, 3 = outside i32 range.
std.parse.i32_status_le: Returns [0x01][i32 LE value] (5 bytes) when the whole input is one decimal with optional single +/- and 0x20/0x09 around it; otherwise the single byte 0x00.
std.parse.i32_status_le_at: Like u32_status_le_at but accepts one leading + or - and enforces i32 range [-2147483648, 2147483647]; the value is two's-complement u32 LE.
std.parse.u32_dec: Parses all of b (0x20/0x09 trimmed at both ends) as unsigned decimal digits; result_i32 err 1 = empty after trim, 2 = non-digit (no sign allowed), 3 = u32 overflow.
std.parse.u32_dec_at: Parses every byte from off to the end of b as decimal digits and returns the u32 value; traps on empty range, any non-digit, or overflow.
std.parse.u32_status_le: Returns [0x01][u32 LE value] (5 bytes) when the whole input is one unsigned decimal with optional 0x20/0x09 around it; otherwise the single byte 0x00.
std.parse.u32_status_le_at: Skips 0x20/0x09 from off, parses 1+ decimal digits (u32 overflow-checked), skips trailing 0x20/0x09; returns [0x01][u32 LE value][u32 LE next_off] (9 bytes) or 0x00 on failure.
std.small_map.empty: Returns an empty map: maps are bytes of concatenated entries [u32 LE key_len][key][LE value] kept sorted by key bytes ascending.
std.small_map.empty_bytes_u32: Returns an empty bytes->u32 map (entries [u32 LE key_len][key][u32 LE value], sorted by key bytes ascending).
std.small_map.get_bytes_u32: Returns key's u32 value, or 0 if the exact key bytes are absent.
std.small_map.get_bytes_u32_or: Returns key's u32 value, or default if the exact key bytes are absent.
std.small_map.get_or: Generic over num_like V: returns key's LE-decoded value, or default if absent (exact key-byte match; early-exits using the sorted order).
std.small_map.inc1: Generic over num_like V: adds 1 (wrapping) to key's value or inserts it with value 1, keeping sorted order; consumes m and returns the new map.
std.small_map.inc1_bytes_u32: Adds 1 (wrapping u32) to key's value or inserts it with value 1, keeping key-bytes-ascending order; consumes m and returns the new map.
std.small_map.iter_init_v1: Returns 0, the starting cursor (a byte offset into the map encoding) for iter_next_v1.
std.small_map.iter_next_v1: Returns a std.doc v1 result: ok payload is the entry bytes [u32 LE klen][key][u32 LE value] (advance the cursor by the payload length); err code 1 = end of map, 2 = negative cursor or truncated entry; assumes 4-byte values.
std.small_map.len: Generic over num_like V: returns the number of entries by walking the map encoding.
std.small_map.len_bytes_u32: Returns the number of entries in a bytes->u32 map by walking its encoding.
std.small_map.put: Generic over num_like V: inserts or replaces key with the LE-encoded value (ty.size_bytes(V) bytes), keeping key-bytes-ascending order; consumes m and returns the new map.
std.small_map.put_bytes_u32: Inserts or replaces key with u32 LE val, keeping key-bytes-ascending order; consumes m and returns the new map bytes.
std.small_map.remove: Generic over num_like V: removes the exactly-matching key's entry; an absent key returns m unchanged; consumes m and returns the map bytes.
std.small_map.remove_bytes_u32: Removes key from a bytes->u32 map by exact byte match; an absent key returns the map unchanged; consumes m and returns the map bytes.
std.text.ascii.first_line_view: Returns the view of bytes before the first 0x0A, or the whole input if none; a trailing 0x0D is not stripped.
std.text.ascii.is_alpha: Returns 1 if c is 0x41-0x5A (A-Z) or 0x61-0x7A (a-z), else 0.
std.text.ascii.is_space_tab: Returns 1 if c is 0x20 (space) or 0x09 (tab), else 0.
std.text.ascii.kth_line_view: Returns a view of 0x0A-separated line k (0-based; k<0 means 0); the tail after the last 0x0A counts as a line; out of range yields an empty view.
std.text.ascii.last_line_view: Returns a view of the last 0x0A-separated line after ignoring one trailing 0x0A; empty input or an empty last line yields an empty view.
std.text.ascii.normalize_lines: Splits at every 0x0A or 0x0D, trims 0x20/0x09 from both ends of each line, drops empty lines, and joins survivors with single 0x0A (no trailing newline).
std.text.ascii.split_lines_view: Splits on 0x0A into an X7SL v1 slices table, dropping one trailing 0x0D per non-empty line; a final 0x0A adds no empty entry; empty input yields 1 empty entry.
std.text.ascii.split_u8: Splits input on byte sep into an X7SL v1 slices table over the input (read with std.text.slices); exactly count(sep)+1 entries, empty segments kept.
std.text.ascii.to_lower_u8: Maps 0x41-0x5A (A-Z) to lowercase by adding 32; any other value is returned unchanged.
std.text.ascii.to_upper_u8: Maps 0x61-0x7A (a-z) to uppercase by subtracting 32; any other value is returned unchanged.
std.text.ascii.tokenize_words_lower: Lowercases A-Z and returns the A-Za-z runs joined by single 0x20 spaces; non-alpha bytes separate words; no leading/trailing space.
std.text.slices.builder_finish_v1: Overwrites the count field (u32 LE at offset 8) with count and returns the builder's bytes branded x7sl_v1; pass the number of entries pushed.
std.text.slices.builder_new_v1: Starts an X7SL v1 builder: vec_u8 seeded with the 12-byte header magic 'X7SL' + u32 LE version 1 + u32 LE count 0; cap_hint<0 is treated as 0.
std.text.slices.builder_push_v1: Appends one entry as u32 LE start then u32 LE len; consumes and returns the builder.
std.text.slices.cast_bytes_v1: Consumes b and returns result_bytes: ok(b branded x7sl_v1) if validate_v1 passes, else err carrying validate_v1's error code (b is dropped).
std.text.slices.copy_at_v1: Returns an owned bytes copy of view_at_v1(base, x7sl, idx), with the same clamping and empty-on-out-of-range behavior.
std.text.slices.count_v1: Returns the table's entry count, read as u32 LE at byte offset 8.
std.text.slices.len_v1: Returns entry idx's length (u32 LE at 16+8*idx); returns 0 if idx is out of range.
std.text.slices.start_v1: Returns entry idx's start offset (u32 LE at 12+8*idx); returns 0 if idx is out of range.
std.text.slices.validate_v1: Checks X7SL v1 layout: ok(count), or err 2119237633 if shorter than 12 bytes, 2119237636 bad magic 'X7SL', 2119237634 version != 1, 2119237635 if total length != 12+8*count.
std.text.slices.view_at_v1: Returns the view base[start..start+len] for entry idx, clamping len to the end of base; out-of-range idx or start yields an empty view.
std.vec.as_bytes: Consumes the builder and returns its contents as owned bytes without copying; the vec_u8 handle is invalidated.
std.vec.extend_bytes: Appends all bytes of view b to the builder; consumes h and returns the updated handle.
std.vec.get: Returns builder byte i as i32 0-255; traps if i >= len.
std.vec.len: Returns the builder's current length in bytes.
std.vec.push: Appends x & 0xFF to the builder, growing capacity by doubling when full; consumes h and returns the updated handle.
std.vec.reserve_exact: Grows capacity to exactly len+additional if currently smaller; traps on u32 overflow; consumes h and returns the updated handle.
std.vec.with_capacity: Creates a new empty vec_u8 byte builder (len 0) with the given capacity.
